name: 15 Social Media Automation Tools for Agencies
header_text: 15 Social Media Automation Tools for Agencies
description:
One of the common challenges you’ll face as an agency is optimizing social posting workflow to consistently deliver high-quality content that drives engagement for your clients. 

If your agency is active on multiple social media platforms, sharing content across all of them can be tedious. 

Thankfully, you can automate your way out of this challenge with social media automation tools. These tools take the grunt work out of various social media management activities.

You can use them to manage repetitive tasks like scheduling posts and generating reports. The tools also help you respond to comments, produce reports, and analyze your performance.

You need to keep a few criteria in mind when you go shopping for a social media automation tool. With the glut of options available, determining the right pick for you can be daunting. 

However, you have a helping hand in the form of this guide. We’ll fill you in with the most important search criteria and provide a list of the top 15 social media automation tools that fulfill critical requirements and then some.

How to choose the best social media automation tool for your agency?

Choosing a social media automation tool is not an easy decision with tons of factors to consider. However, focusing on a few most important factors can help you to get overwhelmed and decide quickly. These factors include:

Supported social platforms

The first thing to look out for is the social media platforms that the tool supports. Most tools cover popular platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and X (formerly known as Twitter). 

Features

Also, check the set of social media features offered for each of the supported platforms. Look for features like those for posting, analytics, and audience engagement. These features are important in determining the true value of a tool.

Ease of use

Ease of navigation is also important to a tool’s usefulness. Evaluate how easy it is to assess the tool’s functions. Choose a tool with a straightforward dashboard that allows you to easily create content, schedule posts, and carry out other related activities. Also, look for search and customization features, as these can further smoothen the learning curve and ease your access to the tool’s features.

Budget and pricing 

It is also important to consider the pricing of the tool with your budget in mind. Watch out for bulk discounts on client slots and pre-paid annual rates. 

Ideally, the pricing should be based on criteria such as the number of users, campaign volumes, and storage allowances, so you can easily calculate the value of what you're paying for. 

Integration with third-party tools 

Also, consider how well the tool integrates with tools in your social media management ecosysem. For example, a social media automation tool that supports analytics apps will enhance accurate reports to track the progress of your social media campaigns. 

Security and data ownership 

Protecting your client's data is very essential. Choose an automation tool that provides user protection measures such as two-step verification and log audits. 

Find out about the tool’s data storage location and other measures applicable to privacy and access control. Also, opt for a tool dedicated primarily to agencies to avoid consumer privacy policies that conflict with your interest as an agency. This helps ensure you’ll have all the feature support to safeguard your clients’ intellectual property with fewer impediments to optimal operations.

Reviews

Reviews help you look beyond what you read on an automation tool’s website. This lets you dig deeper into various aspects of the tool. Check out recent customer reviews about your chosen automation tool to know where it lags and where it excels. 

Choosing the right automation tool can have a great impact on your social media management team's success. The factors listed above will lead you to find the perfect social media automation tool for your team’s peculiar needs. 

15 Social Media Automation Tools for Agencies
